mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a > 0.2pp unemployment jump, or signal loses predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods yield negative 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with rising interest rates forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20-day returns in > 60% of high-inflation, rising-rate environments, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% signals robust economic expansion, prompting immediate equity market repricing and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following > 3.0% GDP prints, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic strength, reducing recession risk and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in industrial output and employment fail to yield positive 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02.
